# Implementation Plan: Create Space

**Branch**: `001-create-space` | **Date**: 2026-05-05 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `specs/001-create-space/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan is generated by the `/speckit.plan` command following Spec Kit workflow.

## Summary

Create a new space (physical location like Home, Office, Dorm, Car) stored locally on device using expo-sqlite. This is the foundational MVP feature enabling users to organize belongings by location. Implementation uses React Native (Expo), TypeScript, and layered architecture: UI → Service (validation) → Repository (parameterized SQL) → expo-sqlite. Scope: < 20 spaces, < 500ms response time, offline-first, simple error handling.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript (React Native, ES2020+)  
**Primary Dependencies**: Expo framework, expo-sqlite, react-native, @react-navigation  
**Storage**: expo-sqlite (production), sql.js (testing)  
**Testing**: Vitest, Jest, @testing-library/react-native  
**Target Platform**: iOS (13+) and Android (API 21+) via React Native  
**Project Type**: Mobile app (Expo-based)  
**Performance Goals**: Space creation < 500ms; list rendering smooth at 60 fps  
**Constraints**: Offline-first; local storage only; no backend; parameterized SQL queries (injection prevention)  
**Scale/Scope**: Single user; < 20 spaces per user; MVP scope (create, read, list, delete)

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

✅ **I. Spec-Driven Development**: Feature spec completed, clarified, ready for planning  
✅ **II. Simplicity First**: No overengineering; MVP scope (create, read, list); no ORM, no cloud sync  
✅ **III. Vertical Slice Development**: UI → Service → Repository → SQLite architecture end-to-end  
✅ **IV. Local-First Architecture**: expo-sqlite, offline-first, single-user, no authentication  
✅ **V. Clean Layered Architecture**: 4-layer design (UI/Service/Repository/DB) with clear boundaries  
✅ **Technology Stack**: React Native + TypeScript + expo-sqlite + parameterized SQL  
✅ **Error Handling**: Simple try-catch, meaningful messages (no complex framework)  
✅ **Testing**: sql.js for in-memory testing (database-agnostic repository)  

**Status**: ✅ **PASS - All constitutional principles satisfied**

```text
    └── requirements.md     # Quality checklist (DONE)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
# Mobile app structure (Expo-based React Native)
app/
├── app/
│   ├── (tabs)/
│   │   ├── index.tsx                # Home screen (spaces list)
│   │   └── spaces/
│   │       ├── [id].tsx             # Space detail screen
│   │       └── create.tsx           # Create space dialog/screen
│   ├── _layout.tsx                  # App layout & navigation
│   └── modal.tsx                    # Modal root
├── src/
│   ├── models/
│   │   └── Space.ts                 # Space entity type definitions
│   ├── services/
│   │   └── SpaceService.ts          # Business logic & validation
│   ├── repositories/
│   │   └── SpaceRepository.ts       # Data access (parameterized SQL)
│   └── db/
│       ├── client.ts                # Database connection (expo-sqlite)
│       └── migrations.ts            # Schema initialization & migrations
└── tests/
    ├── unit/
    │   └── SpaceService.test.ts
    ├── integration/
    │   └── SpaceRepository.test.ts
    └── e2e/
        └── createSpace.test.ts
```

**Structure Decision**: Single Expo mobile app with layered services. No separate backend (offline-first). UI → Service → Repository → SQLite architecture maintained throughout.

## Complexity Tracking

✅ **No constitutional violations**. All design decisions align with Spotly principles:

| Component | Justified | Simpler Alternative | Why More Complex Needed |
|-----------|-----------|-------------------|--------------------------|
| Repository Pattern | ✅ Yes | Direct DB queries in service | Enable database-agnostic testing (sql.js swap) |
| Service Layer | ✅ Yes | Validation in UI | Centralize business logic, reusability, testability |
| Parameterized SQL | ✅ Yes | String interpolation | Security (injection prevention), required by spec |
| TypeScript | ✅ Yes | Plain JavaScript | Type safety, developer experience, catch errors early |

**All complexity is justified and minimal for MVP.**
